zh.lifeSummary: Sir Knight John Wheaton Or Weeden是Joan Bryan的兒子，於1525年出生。他於1552年10月17日與Julyan Strache在英國英格蘭戴文艾可謝特艾可謝特 聖 德洛可結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1633年在英國英格蘭貝德福爾諾迪爾享年108歲。

nameMeanings.name: Wheaton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: History: Robert Wheaton came from England to Rehoboth, MA, c. 1636.

variants.name: Whiddon
meanings: English (Devon): habitational name from any of numerous minor places in Devon named Whiddon. Some are named with Old English hwīt ‘white’ + dūn ‘hill’ or tūn ‘farmstead, estate’; others with Old English hwǣte ‘wheat’ + dūn or denu ‘valley’.

nameMeanings.name: Or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Weeden
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Middlesex, Surrey, and Kent): variant of   Weedon  .
History: James Weeden emigrated from England to Newport, RI, in 1638.

variants.name: Weedon
meanings: English (Buckinghamshire and Hertfordshire): habitational name from Weedon in Buckinghamshire or Weedon Beck and Weedon Lois in Northamptonshire, all of which are named in Old English with wīg, wēoh ‘idol, shrine, (pre-Christian) temple’ + dūn ‘hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: Knight
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status or occupational name from Middle English knight ‘retainer, attendant’ (Old English cniht ‘boy, youth, lad)’. The specialized feudal sense ‘a high-ranking tenant bound to serve his lord as a mounted soldier’ is not known to have ever given rise to the surname, although it is not out of the question that it may occasionally have been used as a nickname, perhaps for someone who played the part of an armed knight in a local pageant.
Irish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the knight’. See also McKnight .

variants.name: Knights
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of Knight , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McKnight
meanings: Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the horseman (Gaelic ridire)’, formerly Anglicized McRuddery.
Scottish (Dumfriesshire and Galloway) and Irish (Ulster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Neachtain, which is usually Anglicized as McNaughton .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: Sir Knight John Wheaton Or Weeden was born in 1525 as the son of Joan Bryan. He married Julyan Strache on 17 October 1552, in Exeter St Petrock, Exeter, Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1633, in Northill, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 108.

Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1633, à Northill, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 108 ans.

ko.Saint Petrock, Exeter, Devon, England: 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 세터 세터 스트 페트록
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Knight John Wheaton Or Weeden(은)는 1525년 Joan Bryan의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1552년 10월 17일 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 세터 세터 스트 페트록에서 Julyan Strache(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1633년 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 노르스이에서 108세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Knight John Wheaton Or Weeden родился в 1525 г. Его мать звали Joan Bryan. Он женился на женщине по имени Julyan Strache 17 октября 1552 г. в местности эксетир ст петрокк, эксетир, девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1633 г. в местности нортилл, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 108 лет.
